description: A full raylib game-loop program: window/FPS setup, a Color shape, a game loop that draws one shape per frame for a fixed number of frames, and a program entry point. Validated against the real transpiler (base form, plus confirmed structurally-varying real examples using different draw calls -- see the coverage note below).

frames.desc: how many frames the demo loop runs before closing

template:
import from C the action InitWindow takes whole number and whole number and text produces nothing as InitWindow
import from C the action CloseWindow takes nothing produces nothing as CloseWindow
import from C the action SetTargetFPS takes whole number produces nothing as SetTargetFPS
import from C the action BeginDrawing takes nothing produces nothing as BeginDrawing
import from C the action EndDrawing takes nothing produces nothing as EndDrawing
import from C the action ClearBackground takes Color produces nothing as ClearBackground
import from C the action DrawCircle takes whole number and whole number and f32 and Color produces nothing as DrawCircle
import from C the action WindowShouldClose takes nothing produces truth value as WindowShouldClose

shape Color holds
    r as byte
    g as byte
    b as byte
    a as byte
end shape

action run_game_loop produces nothing
    keep Background as Color
    set Background.r to 245
    set Background.g to 245
    set Background.b to 245
    set Background.a to 255

    keep BallColor as Color
    set BallColor.r to 255
    set BallColor.g to 0
    set BallColor.b to 0
    set BallColor.a to 255

    call InitWindow with {width} and {height} and "{title}"
    call SetTargetFPS with 60

    keep Frames as whole number with value 0
    keep ShouldClose as truth value with value false

    while Frames is less than {frames} repeat
        call BeginDrawing
        call ClearBackground with Background
        call DrawCircle with 400 and 225 and 40.0 and BallColor
        call EndDrawing
        set Frames to Frames plus 1
        call WindowShouldClose giving ShouldClose
    end while

    call CloseWindow
end action

program {program_name}
    call run_game_loop
end program
requires: A Color field must be set via four separate `set Shape.field to value` statements (r/g/b/a) -- there is no single-statement color-literal syntax.
`call InitWindow` must run before any drawing calls, and `call CloseWindow` must be the last statement in the action.
common_mistakes: COVERAGE NOTE, not a hallucination-fix: this template's window-setup/FPS-loop shape (the {width}/{height}/{title}/{frames}/{program_name} params) is confirmed by real transpiler-validated examples, but those same real examples also swap in different draw calls (DrawPixel, DrawLine, ...) and Color field counts that this template does NOT parameterize -- it fixes one illustrative draw call (DrawCircle) and Color shape. Treat the draw-call body as illustrative, not the only valid option, and don't assume this pattern enumerates every raylib call Dictum supports (see skills/library/gamedev for the fuller raylib binding set).
example:
import from C the action InitWindow takes whole number and whole number and text produces nothing as InitWindow
import from C the action CloseWindow takes nothing produces nothing as CloseWindow
import from C the action SetTargetFPS takes whole number produces nothing as SetTargetFPS
import from C the action BeginDrawing takes nothing produces nothing as BeginDrawing
import from C the action EndDrawing takes nothing produces nothing as EndDrawing
import from C the action ClearBackground takes Color produces nothing as ClearBackground
import from C the action DrawCircle takes whole number and whole number and f32 and Color produces nothing as DrawCircle
import from C the action WindowShouldClose takes nothing produces truth value as WindowShouldClose

shape Color holds
    r as byte
    g as byte
    b as byte
    a as byte
end shape

action run_game_loop produces nothing
    keep Background as Color
    set Background.r to 245
    set Background.g to 245
    set Background.b to 245
    set Background.a to 255

    keep BallColor as Color
    set BallColor.r to 255
    set BallColor.g to 0
    set BallColor.b to 0
    set BallColor.a to 255

    call InitWindow with 800 and 450 and "Dictum Raylib Demo"
    call SetTargetFPS with 60

    keep Frames as whole number with value 0
    keep ShouldClose as truth value with value false

    while Frames is less than 5 repeat
        call BeginDrawing
        call ClearBackground with Background
        call DrawCircle with 400 and 225 and 40.0 and BallColor
        call EndDrawing
        set Frames to Frames plus 1
        call WindowShouldClose giving ShouldClose
    end while

    call CloseWindow
end action

program RaylibDemo
    call run_game_loop
end program
